risk_if_not_implemented: Without Segregation From Enterprise Services, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Protection of Event Logs
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to protect event logs and audit tools from unauthorized access, modification and deletion.
compensating_control_1.justification: Protection of Event Logs (MON-08) provides detective monitoring capability that compensates for the absence of Segregation From Enterprise Services (NET-06.4) by identifying unauthorized, anomalous, or non-compliant activity that the primary control would have prevented. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to ensure network architecture utilizes network segmentation to isolate Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS) to protect from other network resources.
compensating_control_2.justification: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation) (NET-06) provides network-level access restriction that compensates for the absence of Segregation From Enterprise Services (NET-06.4) by limiting attacker reach and lateral movement opportunities across the environment. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
